findings.title: Unrestricted fixed reward can be sybil-drained with dust deposits

best_agent_match.title: Any caller can drain the entire reward reserve by looping tiny boosted deposits

findings.title: Broken fallback lets anyone sweep the remaining AAVE once rewards are nearly exhausted

best_agent_match.title: Fallback branch lets anyone sweep the remaining AAVE balance for free once reserve drops below `REWARD`

findings.title: Pool migrations leave old pools with permanent unlimited AAVE allowance

best_agent_match.title: Pool migrations leave every old pool with a permanent unlimited allowance over reward funds

findings.title: `setPool` accepts zero or EOA addresses, which can black-hole user deposits

best_agent_match.title: Owner can set the pool to an EOA or zero address, causing boosted deposits to silently take user funds without depositing

rejected_candidates.title: Arbitrary `asset` parameter is inconsistent with token handling and can misroute or break deposits
rejected_candidates.reason: The contract only ever transfers and approves AAVE, so non-AAVE deposits are most plausibly expected to revert inside the pool and roll back the whole transaction. The more severe 'miscredited other asset' outcome depends on undocumented pool bugs outside this code.
